Monday before easter . the Monday before easter , be call the holy and great Monday , or the second day of the paschal-week ; and the whole week be call the great week Chrys. to . 5. p 541 , etc , not because the day be long than ordinary , but the blessing be great , because of the great and stupendous blessing not to be comprehend , or utter , which God this week confer upon the world , in the death and resurrection of our bless saviour ; and because it immediate precede the great festival , as easter be call , Joh. 19.31 . or Bern. Ser. 3. in domin . Palmar . because of the four great day in it , viz. the procession of Talm-Sunday , the institution of the bless sacrament on Maundy-Thursday , the passion on Good-Friday , and the continuance in the grave on Saturday , which be the eve to our bless master resurrection . the week also be style the passion-week , the week of fast , dry diet , and penance ( in which the devout sort do eat nothing but bread and salt , and drink nothing but water ) from which strictness , no day be exempt except the lord day , on which it be a great crime to fast Constit. App. l. 5. c . 17. alii . . now then let we every one make a search into our heart , and try whether we find the good spirit of God persuade we indeed , that we be the child of God , and that we have faith : whether we have a feel of spiritual grace : whether the spirit of God have wrought in we a love of God , a hatred of sin , a resolve purpose to amend our life : whether we find in our self , the spirit of grace and of supplication : and whether we can true find that inward conflict between the flesh and the spirit . if these thing be in we , happy be we . but if upon examination have , these thing be want in any , such man may speak of faith , but as yet they have it not : they be far short of true save faith , and so come short of salvation . let the search be make a little more inward : a man may know whether he have faith , by the inward feel of grace ; if faith be in the heart , the spirit of God will set it a work , it will not lie asleep , but it will stir up other grace of God in we ; it will make we to love God , and hate sin , more than ever we do before . it will move we to pray , and call upon the name of the lord . wherefore , if any one do not feel in himself , a gracious work of the good spirit , if he find not in himself a far great love of God , and loathe of sin , if he have not a more holy purpose to obey God , and to do his will ; if he do not , nor can pray , if in his trouble and affliction , outward and inward , he do not seek unto God , if he do not with the child , run to his heavenly father , and cry , Abba , father , it|be a evident sign , that as yet , Christ do not dwell in his heart by faith , and that as yet he live not by the faith of the son of God . let we search yet a little further ; the unregenerate man continue in sin , live in the manifest transgression of the commandment of God , yet presume he have faith . i hope , say he , i have a good faith : but how be it , o man , if thou have true faith , that thou still live in sin ? certain it be , if faith be in thy heart , it will cause a reformation of life , or at least , a holy purpose of amendment of life . and so in time , and by degree , thy heart will be renew , and thy life reform . indeed , i know i ought to reform my way , and amend my life , and i will fain amend , but it be the flesh that draw i to evil . but i demand of thou , o man that complain of the flesh , where be the inward conflict between the flesh and the spirit ? and where be thy strive against thy corruption ? what , do evil thought arise in thy heart , and thou make no resistance ? do thy heart swell with pride ? do filthy lust arise in thy heart ? do thy heart abound with covetous desire ? and overflow with the gall of bitterness , anger , hatred , envy , malice , and desire of revenge ? and do thou not strive against these corruption , but give way unto they , entertain they , and embrace they ? certain , here be no good sign of grace , nor of true faith . for where true faith be , there will be a resist of sin , and a strive against corruption . wherefore , if we will have a good testimony in our self , of the assurance of true faith , than we must be careful to resist sin , and to strive against our corruption : and although the combat will be great , to master our sin , and to get dominion over our corruption , yet by little and little , we shall overcome , and in the end , get the victory . accord to that fay of saint John , 1 Ioh. 5. 4. this be the victory that overcome the world , even our faith .

that if God command that , which by some law of his own be sin , as that Abraham shall kill his son , he be not the author of sin , but only so far , as he command that , which of it self , without that special dispensation of he , be sin ; but by that it cease to be sin . the assumption be false , no protestant defend any such thing ; howsoever we all acknowledge , that it be God will , that Judas shall betray Christ . etc but we deny , that either Judas have any commandment , or warrant from God , or that God put that wicked thought into his heart , or that he incline he to the like of it . neither do we deride any permissive will in God , but that which make he a idle beholder of thing , without any determination of their be , or not be , but only such as d•pend•s whole , or principal upon the creature . we believe , and profess , that God work otherwise by the wicked , then by the godly : in these by put in good thought and bring they to effect , by their will , and labour . in the wicked he do not work , but only by they bring his own purpose to pass , without command , persuade , urge , or impel to sin , this latter you ▪ may ( if you will ) call permission , without fear of be deride by any protestant ;
